km.Northrepps, Norfolk, England: ណូរthរេពស, ណូរប៉ូលក, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.Northrepps, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可諾斯普斯
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge是Rugge的兒子，於1490年出生在英國諾福爾可貝斯頓。他和Helena Windsor至少有1個女兒。他去世於1550年在英國諾福爾可諾斯普斯享年60歲。

zh-hans.Northrepps, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可諾斯普斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge是Rugge的儿子，于1490年出生在英国諾福爾可貝斯頓。他和Helena Windsor至少有1个女儿。他去世于1550年在英国諾福爾可諾斯普斯享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rugge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Elke, Hans, Helmut, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Roger  .
North German and Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a grower of rye or a baker of rye bread, from Low German, Dutch rogge ‘rye’.

variants.name: Roger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Oltrogge
meanings: North German: unflattering nickname for a farmer who sells or provides old rye, from Middle Low German olt ‘old’ + rogge ‘rye’ or just a differentiating name for an older bearer of the (frequent) name Rogge .

variants.name: Rog
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Bronislaw, Jacek, Janusz, Kazimierz, Ryszard, Zofia.
Polish: nickname from róg ‘horn’, or an occupational name for someone whose job was to blow a horn.
Dutch: from rog ‘ray’, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a ray.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge was born in 1490, in Beeston, Norfolk, England as the son of Rugge. He had at least 1 daughter with Helena Windsor. He died in 1550, in Northrepps, Norfolk, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge est né en 1490, à Beeston, Norfolk, Angleterre, fils de Rugge. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Helena Windsor. Il est décédé en 1550, à Northrepps, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge wurde 1490 in Beeston, Norfolk, England, als Sohn von Rugge geboren. Er hatte mit Helena Windsor mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1550 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Northrepps, Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge è nato il nel 1490 nel Beeston, Norfolk, England come figlio di Rugge. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Helena Windsor. Morì nel 1550, nel Northrepps, Norfolk, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.Beeston, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクビーストン
ja.Northrepps, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクノースレップス
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Ruggeは1490年にイギリスノーフォルクビーストンでRuggeの息子として生まれた。彼はHelena Windsorとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1550年にイギリスノーフォルクノースレップスで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge(은)는 1490년 영국 노르폴크 브이스턴에서 Rugge의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Helena Windsor(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1550년 영국 노르폴크 노르스레프스에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge nasceu em 1490, em Beeston, Norfolk, Inglaterra filho de Rugge. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Helena Windsor. Ele faleceu em 1550, em Northrepps, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge родился в 1490 г. в местности бистон, норфолк, Англия. Его отца звали Rugge. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Helena Windsor. Он умер в 1550 г. в местности нортреппс, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rugge nació en 1490, en Beeston, Norfolk, Inglaterra como hijo de Rugge. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Helena Windsor. Murió en 1550, en Northrepps, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años.
